message:
chore(beads): mark xfstests harness task bd-m5wf.7.1 as in_progress

Update issue status to reflect work beginning on the xfstests automated
execution harness for FrankenFS FUSE conformance testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -560,7 +560,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","title":"Add btrfs multi-device and subvolume CLI commands","description":"## Purpose

CLI integration for multi-device mount, subvolume navigation, and snapshot operations.

## Scope

1. `ffs mount --devices /dev/sda,/dev/sdb` — multi-device mount
2. `ffs mount --subvol=<name>` — mount specific subvolume
3. `ffs mount --snapshot=<name>` — mount specific snapshot
4. `ffs inspect --subvolumes` — list all subvolumes
5. `ffs inspect --snapshots` — list all snapshots
6. `ffs inspect --devices` — show device layout and chunk mapping

## Acceptance criteria
- All CLI flags implemented and help text accurate
- Error messages for missing devices, unknown subvolumes, etc.
- Unit tests for CLI argument parsing
- E2E script exercising all new CLI flags","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:26.502295108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T23:25:02.575178883Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T23:25:02.570870001Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added --subvolumes and --snapshots flags to inspect command for btrfs subvolume/snapshot listing. Added --subvol and --snapshot flags to mount command for targeted subvolume/snapshot mounting. inspect_btrfs_subvolumes() walks root tree and uses enumerate_subvolumes/enumerate_snapshots with both human and JSON output. Updated CLI arg parsing and all pattern matches. All 136 ffs-cli tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:26.502295108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:20.352530140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:21.432438155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:22.540965654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.6.5","title":"Verification Gate: btrfs multi-device and subvolume correctness","description":"## Purpose

Verify btrfs feature expansion produces correct results for multi-device and subvolume operations.

## Scope

1. Multi-device chunk mapping correct for RAID0/1/10/5/6
2. Subvolume enumeration and mounting correct
3. Snapshot navigation and diff correct
4. All CLI commands produce correct output
5. All sub-bead tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Multi-device mount E2E passes
- Subvolume mount E2E passes
- Snapshot diff E2E passes
- All new CLI commands tested","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:27.451159258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T23:44:16.815210865Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T23:44:16.814334303Z","close_reason":"Verification gate passed. Added 13 new unit tests in ffs-btrfs: RAID profile identification (8 tests: Single/Dup/Raid0/Raid1/Raid10/Raid5/Raid6/Raid1C3), data_copies, redundancy checks, stripe resolution (4 tests: single/raid1-mirrors/raid0-stripe-selection/no-match), subvolume enumeration (4 tests: user subvols/skip system trees/rdonly flag/fallback name), snapshot enumeration (2 tests: finds snapshots/ignores regular subvols), snapshot diff (5 tests: added/deleted/modified/identical/multiple), ROOT_REF parsing (2 tests). Also added RAID/DEV_ITEM tests in ffs-ondisk. 116 ffs-btrfs tests pass, 136 ffs-cli tests pass, all downstream crates compile.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:27.451159258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:23.641587144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7","title":"EPIC: xfstests Conformance Integration and Automated Runner","description":"## Purpose

Fully integrate xfstests conformance testing. Infrastructure exists (curated manifests, planner/runner automation, CI planning gate) but needs operational integration: automated test execution, pass/fail tracking, regression detection, and continuous improvement.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **xfstests execution harness** — Automated runner that executes the curated subset against a FrankenFS FUSE mount. Parse xfstests output, classify pass/fail/skip/expected-fail. Track results over time.

2. **Pass/fail baseline establishment** — Run full curated subset, establish current pass/fail baseline. Document known failures with root cause analysis.

3. **Regression detection** — CI gate that fails if any previously-passing test regresses. Known failures tracked in allowlist.

4. **Known failure triage and fix** — Investigate and fix known xfstests failures where feasible. Document unfixable failures with rationale.

5. **Verification gate** — Baseline established, CI gate working, known failures documented.

## Non-goals
- Running ALL xfstests (only curated subset relevant to FUSE userspace)
- Fixing kernel-level tests that require kernel features","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:58.022750142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:58.022750142Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:58.022750142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","title":"Implement xfstests automated execution harness","description":"## Purpose

Automated runner that executes xfstests against FrankenFS FUSE mount, parses results, and tracks pass/fail/skip.

## Scope

1. **Test runner** — Shell script that: mounts FrankenFS on temp image, configures xfstests local.config, runs curated subset, captures output.

2. **Result parser** — Parse xfstests output format into structured JSON: test_id, status (pass/fail/skip/not_run), duration, output_snippet.

3. **Allowlist** — JSON file tracking known failures with: test_id, failure_reason, status (known_fail/wont_fix/investigating).

4. **Result comparison** — Compare current run against baseline. Report new passes, new failures, regressions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner executes xfstests curated subset
- Results parsed into structured JSON
- Allowlist mechanism working
- Comparison detects regressions
- Unit tests for result parser
- E2E script: run subset, parse results, compare to baseline","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:50:15.590338Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","title":"Implement xfstests automated execution harness","description":"## Purpose

Automated runner that executes xfstests against FrankenFS FUSE mount, parses results, and tracks pass/fail/skip.

## Scope

1. **Test runner** — Shell script that: mounts FrankenFS on temp image, configures xfstests local.config, runs curated subset, captures output.

2. **Result parser** — Parse xfstests output format into structured JSON: test_id, status (pass/fail/skip/not_run), duration, output_snippet.

3. **Allowlist** — JSON file tracking known failures with: test_id, failure_reason, status (known_fail/wont_fix/investigating).

4. **Result comparison** — Compare current run against baseline. Report new passes, new failures, regressions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Runner executes xfstests curated subset
- Results parsed into structured JSON
- Allowlist mechanism working
- Comparison detects regressions
- Unit tests for result parser
- E2E script: run subset, parse results, compare to baseline","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T05:50:00.054436214Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:24:59.651335399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","title":"Establish xfstests pass/fail baseline with root cause analysis","description":"## Purpose

Run the full curated xfstests subset, establish current baseline, document known failures.

## Scope

1. **Full run** — Execute all tests in curated subset. Record pass/fail/skip for each.
2. **Root cause analysis** — For each failure: identify root cause (FrankenFS bug, FUSE limitation, kernel feature required, test assumption invalid).
3. **Baseline file** — JSON baseline with test_id → expected_status mapping.
4. **Known failures document** — docs/xfstests-known-failures.md with rationale for each failure.

## Acceptance criteria
- Baseline established for full curated subset
- Known failures documented with root cause
- Baseline file committed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:00.614058599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:10:15.540293598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:00.614058599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:25.192137258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","title":"Implement xfstests regression gate for CI","description":"## Purpose

CI gate that detects xfstests regressions: any previously-passing test that now fails blocks the pipeline.

## Scope

1. **Regression check** — Compare current results to baseline. Flag regressions (pass→fail).
2. **New passes** — Report newly-passing tests as positive progress.
3. **Exit code** — Exit 0 if no regressions, exit 1 if regressions detected.
4. **E2E integration** — run_gate.sh compatible.

## Acceptance criteria
- Regression detection working
- Exit codes correct
- E2E script testing regression detection","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:01.553761498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:26.372451384Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","conformance","qa_e2e_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:01.553761498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:26.371368965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","title":"Triage and fix xfstests known failures","description":"## Purpose

Investigate known xfstests failures and fix where feasible.

## Scope

1. Prioritize failures by: severity, frequency of tested operation, feasibility of fix.
2. Fix root causes in FrankenFS code.
3. Document unfixable failures with rationale.
4. Update baseline and allowlist after each fix.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least 50% of known failures investigated
- Fixable failures resolved
- Unfixable failures documented
- Baseline updated","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:03.733643772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:27.581591882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:25:03.733643772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:27.580541323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
